scenario: An operator at Harbor Insurance opens a Camunda 8.8 instance with 1 stuck gateway and needs to change the orderValue variable so it is an integer instead of a string. The operator has located the variable in the instance view and now needs to perform the right gestures to change the value and persist it before retrying.
question: Which icons does the operator click on the instance view to change orderValue and save the new value?

options.text: Click the edit icon next to the orderValue variable, change the value to remove the quotation marks, then click the checkmark icon to save the change
Click the variable name to open a side panel, type the new orderValue value, then click an Apply button at the bottom of the side panel to save
Right-click the orderValue variable to open a context menu, choose Edit value, then press Enter on the keyboard to save the new value to the instance
Click the retry icon next to the orderValue variable, type the new value into the prompt that appears, then click the same retry icon a second time to save

a.text: Correct. The tutorial steps are: click the edit icon next to the variable you want to edit, change the value (here, removing the quotation marks so it is an integer), then click the checkmark icon to save the change.

b.text: Incorrect. Operate's documented gesture uses the edit icon on the variable row and a checkmark icon to save, not a side panel with an Apply button below the variable list.

c.text: Incorrect. The Operate incident tutorial documents an edit icon and a checkmark icon for saving; it does not describe a right-click context menu for editing variables on the instance view.

d.text: Incorrect. The retry control is for re-running the instance after editing, not for editing variables; variable changes use the edit icon and are saved with the checkmark icon.

explanation: To edit a variable on the instance view, the operator clicks the edit icon next to the variable, changes the value (for the example incident, removing the quotation marks so orderValue is an integer), then clicks the checkmark icon to save before triggering a retry.
